memoryArtifacts.headline: Drake, William  story
memoryArtifacts.description: William Drake was born on May 21, 1441, in Great Waltham, Essex, England. He married Joan Mulaine in his hometown. They had nine children in 71 years. He died on December 19, 1510, in his hometown at …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Drake於大約1440年5月21日出生在英國艾可格亞特 沃爾坦時，他的父親Edmund Drake38歲，母親Joan Mylwaye32歲。他於大約1458年與Joan Mulaine在英國艾可格亞特 沃爾坦結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1510年12月19日在他的家鄉享年70歲，埋葬在英國艾可格亞特 沃爾坦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Drake于大约1440年5月21日出生在英国艾可格亞特 沃爾坦时，他的父亲Edmund Drake38岁，母亲Joan Mylwaye32岁。他于大约1458年与Joan Mulaine在英国艾可格亞特 沃爾坦结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1510年12月19日在他的家乡享年70岁，埋葬在英国艾可格亞特 沃爾坦。

nameMeanings.name: Drake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English drake, either ‘drake, male duck’ (compare Duck ) or ‘dragon’ (Old English draca ‘snake, dragon’ or the cognate Old Norse draki), including an emblematic dragon on a flag (compare Dragon ). Both the Old English and the Old Norse forms are from Latin draco ‘snake, monster’; its sense as a nickname is unclear but it may have had the sense ‘standard bearer’. The name was taken to Ireland in the 13th century and reinforced by later English settlers in the 17th century.
German: from Low German drake ‘dragon’, familiar as image on signboards, hence a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn with such signboard.
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized and Flemish, of Draak, a cognate of 2 above, from draak (Middle Dutch drake) ‘dragon’.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

variants.name: Drakes
meanings: English: variant of Drake , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: habitational name from Drax (Yorkshire). The placename probably derives from Old English dræg ‘portage, place where boats are dragged’ or dræg ‘drag net’, influenced by Old Norse drag ‘portage’, with a plural drages being shortened to drags, Drax.
In some cases perhaps an Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) cognate in some other language.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William Drake was born about 21 May 1440, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, his father, Edmund Drake, was 38 and his mother, Joan Mylwaye, was 32. He married Joan Mulaine about 1458, in Great Waltham, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 19 December 1510, in his hometown, at the age of 70, and was buried in Great Waltham, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Drake est né environ 21 mai 1440, à Great Waltham, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Drake, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Joan Mylwaye, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Joan Mulaine environ 1458, à Great Waltham, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 19 décembre 1510, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Great Waltham, Essex, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: William Drakeが約1440年5月21日にイギリスエセックスグレアト ワルサムで生まれた時，父Edmund Drakeは38歳，母Joan Mylwayeは32歳であった。約1458年にイギリスエセックスグレアト ワルサムでJoan Mulaineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1510年12月19日に故郷で70歳で死去し，イギリスエセックスグレアト ワルサムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Drake(이)가 대략 1440년 5월 21일 영국 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Drake(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Mylwaye(은)는 32세였다. 그는 대략 1458년 영국 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 Joan Mulaine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1510년 12월 19일 그의 고향에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 그리트 월섬에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Drake nasceu aproximadamente 21 de maio de 1440, em Great Waltham, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Drake, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Joan Mylwaye, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Mulaine aproximadamente 1458, em Great Waltham, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Drake родился около 21 мая 1440 г. в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edmund Drake было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Mylwaye, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Mulaine около 1458 г. в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 19 декабря 1510 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности грит валтам, эссекс, Англия.
